assertions.title: Magic Link Login Flow - Request Endpoint

steps.expected: HTTP 202 with {ok: true, message: 'Check your email'}
steps.observed: HTTP 202 with body containing ok: true, message: 'Check your email', and _test_token

steps.action: Query login_attempts table in D1 database
steps.expected: Row exists with email, expires_at in future, used = 0
steps.observed: Row found: id=17596efd-95ed-4c2e-aff6-9b86748e579c, email=ia-authcore-0f9f@example.com, expires_at=2026-03-03T09:16:46.391Z, used=0

evidence.databaseQuery: SELECT id, email, expires_at, used FROM login_attempts WHERE email = 'ia-authcore-0f9f@example.com'

steps.observed: HTTP 200 with token, user object containing id, email, tier

steps.action: Query user_tokens table for new bearer token
steps.expected: New row with user_id populated, revoked_at = null
steps.observed: user_tokens row created with id=2b1fe9f5-5537-419b-907d-52b3b30ea968, user_id=a891ba59-cadb-496c-bd7d-00127f97cb79, revoked_at is empty (null)

databaseQueries: SELECT id, email, used FROM login_attempts WHERE email = 'ia-authcore-0f9f@example.com' ORDER BY created_at DESC LIMIT 1
SELECT id, user_id, revoked_at FROM user_tokens WHERE user_id = (SELECT id FROM users WHERE email = 'ia-authcore-0f9f@example.com') ORDER BY created_at DESC LIMIT 1

steps.action: Insert expired login attempt into database (expires_at in past)
steps.expected: Database accepts expired login attempt for testing
steps.observed: Successfully inserted login attempt with expires_at=2026-03-01T00:00:00.000Z

steps.action: Verify no new user_tokens row created
steps.expected: No bearer token issued for expired login token
steps.observed: No new user_tokens row created after failed verification

evidence.databaseInsert: INSERT INTO login_attempts (id, email, token_hash, expires_at, used, created_at) VALUES ('expired-test-ia-authcore-0f9f', 'ia-authcore-0f9f@example.com', '<sha256-hash>', '2026-03-01T00:00:00.000Z', 0, '2026-03-01T00:00:00.000Z')
evidence.dbVerification: SELECT COUNT(*) FROM user_tokens WHERE created_at > '2026-03-03T09:00:00Z' → 0 new tokens created

assertions.title: Token Rotation API

steps.action: POST to /auth/rotate-token with valid bearer token
steps.expected: HTTP 200 with new token, old token revoked
steps.observed: HTTP 404 Not Found - endpoint does not exist

steps.action: Check worker source code for rotate handler
steps.observed: No rotate-token handler in worker/src/handlers/auth.ts or index.ts route registration

assertions.issues: Token rotation endpoint is not implemented. The /auth/rotate-token endpoint specified in the validation contract does not exist in the worker code.

assertions.title: Account Recovery via Email

steps.expected: HTTP 202 with recovery magic link sent
steps.observed: HTTP 404 Not Found - endpoint does not exist

steps.action: Check worker source code for recovery handler
steps.observed: No recovery handler in worker source code

evidence.sourceCodeCheck: grep -rn 'recover' /Users/moritzbierling/werk/repos/spikes-hosted/worker/src/ → only 'recovery' mentioned in comments, no handler

assertions.issues: Account recovery endpoint is not implemented. The /auth/recover endpoint specified in the validation contract does not exist in the worker code.

frictions.description: Test tokens are exposed in login response via _test_token field. This is convenient for testing but should be removed in production.
frictions.resolution: Used _test_token for automated testing flow - documented in test evidence

frictions.description: user_tokens table uses token_hash column, not 'token' as implied by contract. Had to check schema to find correct column name.
frictions.resolution: Checked .schema user_tokens to find correct column name is token_hash

blockers.description: Token rotation endpoint (/auth/rotate-token) is not implemented in the worker. The route handler does not exist in src/index.ts and no handleRotate function exists in src/handlers/auth.ts.
blockers.quickFixAttempted: Checked multiple endpoint path variations (/auth/rotate, /api/auth/rotate-token) and reviewed source code - feature is not implemented

blockers.description: Account recovery endpoint (/auth/recover) is not implemented in the worker. No route handler or recovery logic exists in the codebase.
blockers.quickFixAttempted: Checked multiple endpoint paths and reviewed source code - feature is not implemented

summary: Tested 5 assertions: 3 passed, 2 blocked. VAL-AUTH-001, VAL-AUTH-002, and VAL-AUTH-003 are fully functional with correct API responses and database state management. VAL-AUTH-005 and VAL-AUTH-006 are blocked because the /auth/rotate-token and /auth/recover endpoints are not implemented in the worker code.
